mnemonics: J.A.D.: Java Annotations Describe - Use //, /* */, and /** */ to Document your code
keyPoints: Single-line comments start with // and are for brief explanations on a single line
Multi-line comments are enclosed between /* and */ and can span several lines
Javadoc comments begin with /** and end with */ and are used to generate formal API documentation
commonMistakes: Nesting multi-line comments (e.g., /* ... /* ... */ ... */), which is not allowed and causes a compilation error
Forgetting that // comments only affect the remainder of the line, leading to unintended active code on the same line
Using inconsistent or unclear comments that don't actually explain the 'why' behind the code, making them useless for future maintenance
